title: Isolation of ribosome-associated nascent chains of soluble proteins produced in Escherichia coli
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"15prr","text":"Isolation of ribosome-nascent chains of co-translationally folded domains. Detailed description of protocol published in ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"264h","text":"https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.1810523115. The arrest sequence used for stalling was TnaC (the leader peptide of the tryptophanase operon). guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"e92hm","text":"Protocol can be scaled up to obtain higher yields for biochemical experiments. It can also be adapted for isolating nascent chains of membrane proteins in complex with translocons. Please get in touch with the authors if needed. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f3mbg","text":"I recommend performing expression tests to pick clones that give the best yields. before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"d5dg1","text":"Use a tuned-expression system for production. This study uses a plasmid under the control of an arabinose promotor, but any expression system (e.g. lac operon) may be used. The inducer needs to be adapted according to the expression vector used. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dpice","text":"Engineering of the construct and the vector has not been included in this protocol. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bci94","text":"Use the Escherichia coli KC6 strain to efficiently arrest translation of TnaC. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Expression tests (perform after transformation of constructs into KC6 cells) Day 1 Pick 5 single clones from plates and seed into 2 ml LB supplemented with antibiotic of choice. Grow overnight at 37°C by shaking at 200 rpm. Prepare a master plate seeded with each of the clones used. Day 2 Sub-culture the overnight cultures into 50 ml of LB supplemented with antibiotics to an A600 of 0.1. Culture at 37°C till it reaches an A600 of 0.5. (Check A600 every 30 min). After cell growth to an A600 of 0.5, add 0.2% Arabinose (final concentration) to the cultures to induce expression and culture at 37 °C for 01:00:00 Cool cultures on ice for 00:15:00 Collect cells by centrifugation 00:10:00 at 4 °C Resuspend each cell pellet in 5 mL buffer A. Transfer the 5 mL of cell suspensions into several 2 ml reaction tubes. Supplement with 10mg/ml lysozyme and 10 mg/ml DNAse1. Lyse cells by repeated cycles of freezing and thawing. (Plunge reaction tubes into liquid nitrogen to freeze and transfer tubes to a thermoblock at 42°C to thaw. Repeat 10 times and mix the lysate well between cycles of freezing and thawing. Spin down lysate for 00:30:00 at in a cooled table-top centrifuge at 4 °C . Transfer the lysate to ultracentrifuge tubes (polycarbonate tubes suited for the Beckman TLA 100.3 ultracentrifuge), underlie with Buffer B using a syringe and needle carefully to avoid bubbles. (1 ml of lysate and 1 ml of Buffer B per tube). Centrifuge at for 01:00:00 in a TLA 100.3 rotor. Discard supernatant after centrifugation the ribosome go into the pellet. Resuspend ribosomal pellet in 30 ul buffer C. Measure concentration on a nanodrop/spectrophotometer at A260. Load different dilutions on SDS-PAGE (A260 of 7 for coomassie and A260 of 14 for Western blotting). Check for differences in expression and make a reserach cell bank (glycerol stocks) of the best expressing clone. If expression is not sufficient, check more clones or improve expression conditions.

Purification of ribosome-associated nascent chains of soluble proteins Day 1 Prepare 2.1 litres of lysogeny broth (use high purity yeast extract and tryptone as described in the Methods section). I divided the medium into four flasks of 500 ml each. Prepare one flask with 100 ml for the seed culture. Autoclave. Prepare buffers. Seed the best expressing clone into 100 ml LB supplemented with antibiotic. Grow overnight at 37°C. Day 2 Dilute the overnight cultures into the flasks containing 500 ml of LB (supplemented with antibiotic for selection) to an A600 of 0.1. Culture at 37°C to an A600 of 0.5. Check for growth every 00:30:00 Induce expression of plasmid with 0.2% arabinose for 1 hour. Chill cultures on ice for 30 min. Collect cells by centrifugation for 00:10:00 at 4 °C in a JLA 8.1000 rotor. Measure cell mass (wet weight of the pellet). Resuspend cells in 2 ml BufferA/g cell mass. Lyse cells with an emulsifex (3 passes at 8000 psi). Clarify the cell lysate by centrifugation ( for 00:30:00 at 4 °C ) in a JA25-50 rotor. Distribute the cell lysate into Ti70 ultracentrifuge tubes (Beckman Coulter) (fill to around 12 mL ) and underlie with 12 mL of buffer B with a needle and syringe. Centrifuge at for 20:00:00 at 4°C in a Ti70 rotor. Day 3 Discard supernatant and gently resuspend the ribosome pellet in Buffer A ( 5 mL ). Keep on ice and shake gently till the pellet goes into solution. While the pellet is on ice, prepare the Talon resin. Swirl the bottle to resuspend the resin, pipette out 1 ml in a 15 ml tube and wash thrice with 10 bed volumes of buffer A. Supplement the last wash with 10 ug/ml total E.coli tRNA. Transfer the ribosomal suspension to a dounce homogeniser and use the loose piston to homogenise. Transfer the homogenate to the washed Talon resin and incubate for 01:00:00 for binding (rotate the tube on a roller). Centrifuge at for 2 min, discard supernatant. Wash with 5x 10 bed volumes of wash buffer. Elute with 2 bed volumes of elution buffer (incubate for 00:10:00 to 00:15:00 min with elution buffer) before collecting the elution. Transfer eluant to polycarbonate ultracentrifuge tubes suited for the Beckman Coulter TLA 100.3 rotor, and spin at for 02:00:00 . Discard the supernatant by pipetting out immediately, and resuspend the 70S pellet obtained in 20 µL buffer C. (I recommend a small volume to have a concentrated sample that can be easily diluted for further use. Make small aliquots since it's best to avoid multiple freeze-thaw cycles). Resuspension to be carried out by adding requisite volume to the pellet and letting the pellet go into solution. Plunge freeze in liquid nitrogen and store at -80 °C long-term.
creator: Renuka Kudva
